libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property setup appears necessary. Prefer in-process tests of XML serialization/parsing, WebDAV method/request objects, headers, properties, reports, locks, and servlet-facing context classes using small local servlet request/response stubs rather than a live WebDAV server.

deterministic_setup.reason:
Classes such as AbstractWebdavServlet, WebdavRequestImpl, OutputContextImpl, InputContextImpl, and several header/request APIs expose javax.servlet and javax.servlet.http types. Meaningful unit coverage of the WebDAV server/request-context surface needs these types on the test compile/runtime classp
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact is old and depends on Java 5-era libraries such as commons-httpclient 3.0 and SLF4J 1.5.x; avoid adding newer framework dependencies unless local Gradle verification requires them.
Servlet-facing tests may need simple local stubs or mocks, but a container or external server is not required for meaningful coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jackrabbit-webdav is a WebDAV client/server utility library whose artifact has servlet-based public APIs, but its POM declares javax.servlet:servlet-api only as a provided dependency, so Gradle will not bring it into generated tests transitively.
